questions.question:
3. [11-12 Standardized Test 1, 6]
John can walk 4 km every hour and run 5 km every hour. He starts from his home and walks 
to the north for 1.2 hours. Then he runs to the south for 0.5 hour. After taking a rest, he 
walks to the south for 1 hour. If the positions on the north of his home are represented by 
positive numbers, use a directed number to represent his final position. (3 marks)
questions.answer:
3. [11-12 Standardized Test 1, 6]
The directed number to represent his final position is
4  1.2 – 5  0.5 – 4  1 
= 4.8 – 2.5 – 4 
= –1.7 (km)

questions.question:
7. [12-13 Standardized Test 1]
Amy measures her body temperature every day and her body temperature is 36.5°C on Monday. 
She takes an increase as a positive number and records the following changes in her body 
temperature from Tuesday to Friday.
Day Tuesday Wednesday Thursday Friday
Temperature change (°C) – 0.5 + 1.8 – 2.1 + 3.4
(a) Use a directed number to represent the total change in Amy’s body temperature from 
Tuesday to Friday. (2 marks)
(b) Students are not recommended to go to school if their body temperature is above or equal 
to 38°C. Should Amy go to school on Friday? Ex
questions.answer:
7. [12-13 Standardized Test 1]
(a) The total change
 = – 0.5 + 1.8 – 2.1 + 3.4-----1A
 = 2.6 (°C)
(b) Amy’s temperature on Friday
 = 36.5 + 2.6
 = 39.1 (°C)
 > 38 (°C)
 \
No, she should not go to school.
https://www.study-together.com/edu/
TB(1A) Ch. 1 Directed Numbers & the Number Line GHS Past Paper Question Bank – Solutions Page 4 of 6

questions.question:
9. [12-13 Mid-year 7]
The temperatures of city A and city B are 5˚C and –3˚C respectively today afternoon. If the 
temperature of city A remains the same, while that of city B decreases by 7˚C at night, what is 
the difference between the temperatures of cities A and B at night? (3 marks)
questions.answer:
9. [12-13 Mid-year 7]
The temp. of city B at night 
= (–3 – 7) ˚C = – 10 ˚C
The difference between the temp. of cities A & B at night
= [5– (– 10)] ˚C
= (5 + 10) ˚C
= 15 ˚C
questions.source_file: Good-Hope-School_(11-16) 1A-Ch.1-Directed Numbers _ the Number Line - CQ.pdf

questions.question:
12. [13-14 Standardized Test 1]
 The temperature (°C) of a room is measured every hour from 09:00 to 15:00. The change of 
temperature in each hour was recorded as follows.
+0.4°C, +0.9°C, +1.5°C, –0.5°C, –1°C, –1.3°C. 
“+0.4°C” represents that the temperature increased by 0.4°C from 9:00 to 10:00.
It is known that the temperature recorded at 9:00 was 28.7°C.
(a) Find the highest temperature recorded. (2 marks)
(b) Chloe claims, “the lowest temperature was recorded at two different times.” Do you 
agree? Explain briefly. (3 marks)
questions.answer:
12. [12-13 Standardized Test 1]
 (a) The highest temperature 
 = 28.7 + (+0.4) + (+0.9) + (+1.5) 
 = 31.5(℃)
 (b) Method 1
The lowest temperature
= 31.5 + (–0.5) + (–1) + (–1.3)
= 28.7(℃)
At 09:00 and 15:00, the temperature 
record is the lowest.
Yes, I agree.
 Method 2
The lowest temperature 
= 31.5 + (+0.4) + (+0.9) + (+1.5) + (–0.5) + (–1)
+ (–1.3)
= 28.7(℃)
At 09:00 and 15:00, the temperature 
record is the lowest.
Yes, I agree.
questions.source_file: Good-Hope-School_(11-16) 1A-Ch.1-Directed Numbers _ the Number Line - CQ.pdf

and 2 blank answers. (4 marks)

questions.question:
14. [14-15 Mid-year Exam]
The total number of $10-notes and $20-notes is 100. The total value of the notes is 
$1760. Find the number of $10-notes and $20-notes respectively. (4 marks)
questions.answer:
14. [14-15 Mid-year Exam]
Let y be the number of $10-notes.
10y  20(100 y) 1760 10y  200020y 1760 10y  240 y  24
There are 24 $10-notes.
The number of $20-notes
= 100-24
= 76

questions.question:
1. [11-12 Mid-year, 2]
Carmen has
2x
$100-notes and 
(3x 1)
$50-notes. 
(a) Find the total amount of the notes in terms of x. (2 marks)
(b) Hence, find the total amount of money that Carmen has if x = 3. (2 marks)
questions.answer:
1. [11-12 Mid-year, 2]
 (a) Case 1:
Total amount of the notes 
 = $2x100(3x 1)50
= $(200x + 150x – 50)
= $(350x – 50)
Case 2:
Total amount of the notes 
 = 2x + 3x – 1 
= 5x – 1
(b) Case 1:
The total amount of money that Carmen has 
 = $(350 × 3 – 50)
 = $1 000
Case 2:
If x = 3, the total amount of notes
= 5(3) – 1 
= 14
If x = 3, the total amount of money
= 2x(100) + (3x – 1)(50) 
= 2(3)(100) + [3(3) – 1](50) 
= 600 + 400
= ($)1 000

questions.question:
2. [11-12 Mid-year, 6]
There are altogether 10 $2.2-stamps and $1.4-stamps. If the total value of the stamps is $16.4, 
find the number of $2.2-stamps. (4 marks)
questions.answer:
2. [11-12 Mid-year, 6]
Let x be the number of $2.2-stamps.
Then the number of $1.4-stamp = 10 – x. 
2.2x 1.4(10 x) 16.4
2.2x 141.4x 16.4
0.8x  2.4
x  3

There are 3 $2.2-stamps.

15. [15-16 Mid-year Exam Q6]
The length of a rectangle is 5 cm more than twice of its width. Find the width and the length of 
the rectangle if the perimeter of the rectangle is 70 cm. (4 marks)
questions.answer:
15. [15-16 Mid-year Exam Q6]
Let x cm be the width, (5 + 2x) cm be the length of the rectangle.
2[x + (5 + 2x)] = 70
 2 (3x + 5) = 70
 3x + 5 = 35
 3x = 30
 x = 10
∴ The width is 10 cm and the length is 25 cm.

= 76

 If the sum of three consecutive odd numbers is 279, find the smallest number. (3 marks)
questions.answer:
11. [16-17 Mid-year Exam Q10] 
Let x be the smallest integer. 
Then, the other two integers are x + 2 and x + 4. 
x + (x + 2) + (x + 4) = 279 
 x + x + 2 + x + 4 = 279
 3x = 273 
 x = 91 
∴ The smallest integer is 91.

questions.question:
10. [18-19 Standardized Test #5]
A pen cost $ x. A rubber is $3 cheaper than the pen.
(a) Express the total cost of a dozen of rubbers in terms of x. (1 mark)
(b) It is known that x is a positive integer. Is it possible that the cost of a dozen of rubbers 
is $54? (2 marks)
questions.answer:
10. [18-19 Standardized Test #5]
(a) Total cost of a dozen of rubbers = $12(x – 3) 1A (accept $(12x – 36))
(b)
12x 36  54
 12x  90
 x  7.5
x is not an integer.
 It is not possible.
1M 
1f.t.

questions.question:
11. [18-19 Standardized Test #6]
Alice poured some water at 25°C into a container. She then put the container into a 
refrigerator to make ice. The temperature change of water is 3°C in the first hour and 5°C 
every hour afterwards in the refrigerator.
(a) What is the temperature of the water 4 hours later? (1 mark)
(b) The temperature of ice will reach –13°C x hours later. Find the value of x. (2 marks)
questions.answer:
11. [18-19 Standardized Test #6]
(a) Temperature of water 4 hours later 
= 25 + (3) + (5)  3
= 25  3  15
= 7 (°C) 1A
(b) 25 + (3) + (5)  (x  1) = –13
 22  5x + 5 = –13
 5x = –40
 x = 8
1M accept trial-and-error method to 
attain the answer
1A
questions.source_file: Good-Hope-School_(16-21) 1A-Ch. 4 Linear Equations in One Unknown- CQ.pdf

questions.question:
12. [18-19 Standardized Test #7]
Alan, Ben and Calvin share 60 sweets among them. The number of sweets Alan gets is 5 more 
than the number of sweets Ben gets. The number of sweets Ben gets is 10 less than twice the 
number of sweets Calvin gets. Calvin claims that he gets more sweets than Alan. Do you 
agree? Explain your answer. (3 marks)
questions.answer:
12. [18-19 Standardized Test #7]
Let x be the number of sweets Calvin gets. Then Ben 
gets (2x – 10) sweets, and Alan gets (2x – 10 + 5) 
sweets.
x  (2x 10)  2x 105
= 60
5x − 15 = 60
x = 15
When x = 15,
2x – 5 = 30 – 5 = 25
∴ Calvin gets 15 sweets and Alan gets 25 sweets.
∵ 15 < 25
∴ The claim is disagreed
1M for setting correct equation
1M using substitution to find the 
amount of the other one
1f.t. must include comparison
questions.source_file: Good-Hope-School_(16-21) 1A-Ch. 4 Linear Equations in One Unknown- CQ.pdf

questions.question:
30. [20-21 Final Exam, #14]
 Janice and Karen shared some books. Originally, Karen has 20 books less than Janice. After 
Janice buying 30 extra books for herself, the number of books that Janice has now became 
twice that of Karen. Find the number of books owned by Janice originally. (3 marks)
~ End ~
https://www.study-together.com/edu/
questions.answer:
30. [20-21 Final Exam, #14]
 Let the original number of books owned by Janice be J. 
 Then the number of books owned by Karen is (J – 20). 1M for correct relationship
 J + 30 = 2(J – 20) 1A
 J + 30 = 2J – 40
 J = 70
 Janice had 70 books originally. 1A
~ End ~
https://www.study-together.com/edu/

questions.question:
3. [12-13 Mid-year 11]
In a 5-set tennis match, the playtime (in min) of each set is as below: 
15.06 45.87 24.12 36.79 20.91
(a) Estimate the total playtime of the match by
(i) rounding off the playtime of each set correct to the nearest 5 min first. (3 marks) 
(ii) using the compensation strategy. (2 marks) 
(b) Which of the two estimation strategies in (a) gives a more accurate estimate? Explain 
your 
answer briefly. (1 mark)
questions.answer:
3. [12-13 Mid-year 11]
(a) (i) The total playtime is
15.06 + 45.87 + 24.12 + 36.79 + 20.91 
= 15 + 45 + 25 + 35 + 20, cor. to the nearest 5 min
= 140 (min)
(ii) The total playtime is
= (15 + 45 + 24 + 36 + 20) + 
 (0.06 + 0.87) + (0.12 + 0.79) + 0.91 
140 + 1 + 1 + 1 
= 143 (min)
(b) Method 1:
The compensation strategy gives a more accurate estimate because it uses the sum of all the original integral 
parts of the numbers and the estimated sum of all of their decimal parts in the calculation.
Method 2:
The actual total playtime is
15.06 + 45.87 + 24.12 + 36.79 + 20.91 
= 142.7

marking_scheme.note: Method: The compensation strategy gives a more accurate estimate because it uses the sum

questions.question:
10. [20-21 S.2 Standardized Test, #7]
A tablet is considered to be qualified if its weight is measured as 800 g correct to the nearest g.
(a) Find the least possible weight of a qualified tablet. (2 marks)
(b) Is it possible that the total weight of 48 qualified tablets is measured as 38.3 kg correct 
to the nearest 0.1 kg? Explain your answer. (3 marks)
questions.answer:
10. [20-21 S.2 Standardized Test, #7]
(a) The maximum absolute error = 0.5 g
The least possible weight of a qualified tablet
= 800 g – 0.5 g
= 799.5 g
(b) Method 1
The least possible weight of 48 qualified tablets
 799.5g48  38376g
 38.376 kg
> 38.35 kg
Thus, it is impossible that the total weight of 48 qualified tablets is measured as 38.3 
kg correct to the nearest 0.1 kg.
Method 2
Note that
48
38.35 kg

 0.798958 ...kg 798.958...g
 799.5g
Thus, it is impossible that the total weight of 48 qualified tablets is measured as 38.3 
kg correct to the nearest 0.1 kg.

marking_scheme.note: Method: (a) The maximum absolute error = 0.5 g

questions.question:
1. [11-12 Standardized Test 2 Q3] 
In Figure 2, A(–3, 5), B(–8, –2), C(–8, –5) and 
D(6, –2) are the vertices of a quadrilateral. BD cuts 
the y-axis at P. 
(a) Write down the coordinates of P. (1 mark) 
(b) Find the area of quadrilateral ABCD. (3 marks)
questions.answer:
1. [11-12 Standardized Test 2 Q3]
(a) P = (0, -2)
(b) Draw AE  BD at E(-3, -2). 
OR Refer to the figure. -----Deduct PP if missing
BD = 6 + 8 = 14 (units)
AE = 5 + 2 = 7 (units)
BF = -2 + 5 = 3 (units)
Area of quadrilateral ABCD
= 14 × 7 ÷ 2 + 14 × 3 ÷ 2
= 49 + 21
= 70 (sq. units)

marking_scheme.note: Method: (b) Draw AE  BD at E(-3, -2)

 (3 marks)
questions.answer:
5. [12-13 Standardized Test 2 Q4]
Construct a perpendicular line from A to meet CB produced at P(1, –1). OR Refer to the fig.
BC = [–1 – (– 4)] units = 3 units
AP = [2 – (– 1)] units = 3 units
The area of ΔABC 
= (BC)(AP) ÷ 2
= (3)(3) ÷ 2 sq. units
= 4.5 sq. units

11. [20-21 Standardized Test, #7]
The marked prices of a pack of rice of brand A and brand B are both $160. If they are sold at a 
discount of 25%, the loss percent from selling a pack of rice of brand A is 20%.
(a) Find the cost prices of a pack of rice of brand A. (3 marks)
(b) It is given that the cost price of a pack of rice of brand B is $100. If a pack of rice of brand 
A and a pack of rice of brand B are sold, find the overall profit or loss percent. (2 marks)
questions.answer:
11. [20-21 Standardized Test, #7]
(a) Selling price of a pack of rice = $160 × (1 – 25%) 1M
 = $160 × 0.75
 = $120
Let $x be the cost price of a pack of rice of brand A.
x × (1 – 20%) = 120 1M
x = 120  0.8
= 150
∴ The total cost price of a pack of rice of brand A is $150. 1A
(b) Percentage change 
= 1M
= –4%
∴ The loss percent is 4%. 1A

 = 180°

 E = 124o

∴ AG // EF (corr. s equal) 
∵ AB // CD 
∴ CD // EF

In Figure 7, ABCDEFGHI is a 9‐sided polygon.  
ABGHI is a pentagon, BCDEFG is a hexagon and BG // IH.
(a) Find x and y. (4 marks) 
(b) Prove that BC // GF. (2 marks)
questions.answer:
7. [13-14 Standardised Test 2, #8] 
(a) 2x + x – 15o = 180o (int. s, HI // BG)    
             x = 65o
          
  2x + x – 15o + y + 15o + 140o + 155o  
      = (5 – 2) × 180o ( sum of polygon)     
130o + 65o + y + 140o + 155o = 540o  
                    y = 50o      
(b) CBG + BGF + 156o + 2y + 4x + 24o = (6 – 2) × 180o
  ( sum of polygon)         
   CBG + BGF + 180o + 2(50o
) + 4(65o) = 720o
∵ CBG + BGF = 180o  
 ∴ BC // GF (int. s supp.)

questions.question:
12. [15-16 Final Exam, #8] 
An n‐sided convex polygon is given.
     (a) Express the sum of interior angles of the polygon in terms of n. (1 mark)
   (b) It is known that one of the interior angles of this polygon is x and the sum of all the other interior angles is
1000o
.
(i) Write down the sum of interior angles of this polygon in terms of x.   (1 mark)
(ii) Show that x = 180o
n – 1360o
.   (1 mark)
(iii) It is known that x < 180o
. Write down the value of n.   (1 mark)
~ End ~ 
https://www.study-together.com/edu/
questions.answer:
12. [15-16 Final Exam, #8] 
(a) (n – 2)180o  (∠sum of polygon)
       (b) (i) 1000o + x           
           (ii)     (n – 2)180o = 1000o + x  
            180o
n – 360o
   = 1000o + x
                 180o
n   = 1360o + x  
                     x   = 180o
n – 1360o   
     (b) (iii)  n = 3, x < 0
            …..
            n = 7, x < 0
            n = 8, x > 0
            n = 8                    
~ End ~

questions.question:
6. [18-19 Standardized Test 2 Q2]
A block of chocolate cuboid of dimension 15 cm  18 cm  20 cm is melted and moulded into 
N pieces of smaller chocolate cuboids of dimension 3 cm × 3 cm × 4 cm.
(a) Write down the value of N. (1 mark)
(b) Find the total surface area of all the smaller chocolate cuboids. (2 marks)
questions.answer:
6. [18-19 Standardized Test 2 Q2]
(a) N = (15 ÷ 3) × (18 ÷ 3) × (20 ÷ 4) = 150 1A
(b) Total surface area of each smaller chocolate cuboid 
= (3 × 3) × 2 + (3 × 4) × 4 = 66 cm2
Required total surface area = 66 × 150 = 9900 cm2
1A

Given that the perimeter of the triangle in the diagram is 30 cm, find the area of the triangle.
A. 
2 60cm B. 
2 65cm C. 
2 30cm D.
2 78cm
【知識點】三角形面積
【答案】C
【題解】三角形周界是三條邊長總和，所以另一條直角邊是 30-12-13=5cm，三角形面積=底×高÷
2=5×12÷2=30 cm2。

questions.text:
下圖中，O 點和 P 點是兩個大小相同的圓的圓心，每個圓的直徑都是 12 厘米，陰影部分的周界
是多少厘米？
In the figure below, points O and P are the centers of two identical circles, each with a diameter of 12 cm. 
What is the perimeter of the shaded area in centimeters?
A. 
12 B. 
18 C. 
36 D.
72
【知識點】圓的特性
【答案】B
【題解】陰影三角形是一個等邊三角形，OP 既是圓的半徑，也是三角形的邊長。半徑
=12÷2=6cm，所以三角形周界是：6×3=18cm。

小耀在計算
0.37 0.8 

37 8

第 8 頁，共 17 頁
香港學而思教研團隊出品
When Tony was calculating 0.37×0.8, he accidentally calculated it as 37×8 by mistake. The answer he 
obtained is how many times the correct answer?
A. 
10 B. 
100 C. 
1000 D.
10000
【知識點】小數乘法
【答案】C
【題解】0.37 當作 37，小數點往右移動兩位，相當於擴大 100 倍，0.8 當作 8，小數點往右移動
一位，相當於擴大 10 倍，所以計算結果比正確答案擴大 100×10=1000 倍。

questions.text:
估算平均數
3. 平均數應用題
單元三：平均數
估計23、9、0、40、18的平均數。
6人平均體重是48.8公斤，其中5人的體重分別是40.2公斤，44.6公斤，52.3公斤，55.8公斤和60.4公
斤。餘下一人的體重是多少公斤？
6人總體重=人數×平均體重
6 × 48.8 = 292.8 (公斤)
餘下一人的體重=6人總體重-其他5人總體重
292.8 - (40.2 + 44.6 + 52.3 + 55.8 + 60.4) = 39.5 (公斤)

questions.text:
平均數
找出23、9、0、40、18的平均數。
(23 + 9 + 0 + 40 + 18) ÷ 5
( 0 + 40 ) ÷ 2 = 20
= 90 ÷ 5
= 18
平均數=總和÷項數
總和=平均數×項數
項數=總和÷平均數
知道其中兩個量即可求出另外一個量。
估計平均數=（最大數+最小數）÷ 2
解答平均數應用題時，分析平均數、項數和總數，知道其中兩個量即可求出第三個
量。再分析問題，求出最後答案。

單元十一：時間和速率

開始時間=結束時間-活動時間
速率是物體在一個時間單位（例：1小時、1秒）內一定的距離。移動越快，速率越高。

questions.text:
行程圖
行程圖是記錄行程的圖像，閱讀行程圖可知道所走的路程、速率和時間等。
例：思思往郵局收取郵包，然後回家。下面是思思的行程圖。
路程：50米
時間：10秒
50 ÷ 10 = 5
速率是5米每秒。
速率：5米每秒
時間：10秒
5 × 10 = 50
路程是50米。
路程：50米
速率：5米每秒
50 ÷ 5 = 10
時間是10秒。
速率 = 路程 ÷ 時間 路程 = 速率 × 時間 時間 = 路程 ÷ 速率

questions.text:
從行程圖中比較速率
19
例：比較甲乙丙三人速率的大小。
甲乙路程相同，但甲用的時間比乙少，則甲的速率比乙高。
乙丙的時間相同，但乙的路程比丙多，則乙的速率比丙高。
觀察圖中可知，斜線的傾斜度由大到小：甲＞乙＞丙
則速率由大到小：甲＞乙＞丙
行程圖中，線段愈斜，表示相關的速率愈高。 ~

questions.text:
每週由年級資深教師負責組織備課，講解知
識點及例題；

集體討論環節：結合本地主流教材，根據班
上小朋友實際情況討論出最適合孩子的教學
方法；

錄製課前預習視頻，讓小朋友提早了解學習
內容。
課中

由主講老師帶領小朋友學習新知識點，從原
理上理解數學概念，學習解題思路和方法技
巧；

課堂練習：精選校內高頻考點和題型，由淺
入深，確保小朋友充分掌握知識重難點，提
升解題速度和正確率；

出門測：根據校內進度每週進行綜合測評，
及時發現小朋友薄弱環節，提供個性化反饋
與跟進。
課後

分級課後練習：課後練習題按難度分為3個
等級，適合不同程度的孩子及時複習所學內
容，並由老師進行批改和講解。

計算能力專項提升：每月派發一本《計算小
超市》，涵蓋整數、分數、小數及方程等多
種類型的計算練習，旨在提高學生的計算速
度與準確性。

結合香港主流數學教輔題目及名校考試真題，由淺入深，循序漸
進。幫助學生鞏固基礎，逐步挑戰難題，培養邏輯思維與應試能
力，讓學生熟悉多樣題型，從容應對各類考試。
引導孩子自主思考，培養數理思維能力。
